faculty: Faculty of Health and Life Sciences
overview: The Environmental Science & Disaster Management (ESDM) is a multidisciplinary program, based on social science, humanities and sciences. Students can develop innovative interdisciplinary approaches to understand the environment and human lives and their relationships.
waiver_info: Result-based waiver (Bachelor's), Waiver on SGPA (Semester result), Female quota, Need-based, Quota position, Physically challenged/specially-abled, tribal/ethnic group quota, Sibling/spouse and son/daughter of current students quota, Waiver for DIU alumni, Group admission quota, Freedom fighter quota, And many more.
eligibility: Students having minimum 2.5 GPA individually or second division in both SSC and HSC, or equivalent background study for admission into Environmental Science & Disaster Management program
labs: Instrumental Methods for Environmental Analysis (with Lab)
curriculum_highlights: Introduction to Geographic Information System (with Lab)
Remote Sensing for Environmental Analysis (with Lab)
Instrumental Methods for Environmental Analysis (with Lab)
